# Cuko Ltd — Fractional Hands-On CTO

> Cuko Ltd is the consulting vehicle of Samuel Ventimiglia, a London-based fractional Chief Technology Officer. The company offers hands-on, compliance-aware engineering leadership to crypto-native finance firms, healthcare platforms, AI and agent-infrastructure startups, and cloud-native scale-ups.

> **Unique market position:** the only UK independent operator implementing DORA (Regulation (EU) 2022/2554), MiCA (Regulation (EU) 2023/1114), and the EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) at article level while shipping production code. EU AI Act high-risk system obligations apply 2 August 2026.

## Entity

- **Name**: Cuko Ltd
- **Type**: Private company limited by shares
- **Jurisdiction**: England and Wales, United Kingdom
- **Companies House number**: 14989953
- **Registered office**: 58 Twyford Avenue, W3 9QB, London, United Kingdom
- **Founder and operator**: Samuel Ventimiglia
- **Founded**: 2026
- **Website**: https://cuko.uk
- **Primary email**: samuel@cuko.uk

## Operator

- **Name**: Samuel Ventimiglia
- **Title**: Fractional Chief Technology Officer
- **Location**: London, United Kingdom
- **Languages**: English, Italian
- **Experience**: 17+ years in senior engineering leadership
- **Production launches**: 40+ shipped systems across four industries
- **Scale work**: 4 products carried from constrained beta to worldwide scale
- **Incident experience**: 3 production data-breach engagements handled end-to-end including regulator and board communication
- **Certifications**: AWS, LangChain

## Sectors served

- Crypto-native finance — funds, OTC desks, exchanges, DeFi protocols
- Healthcare — clinical platforms, data-sensitive integrations
- AI and agent infrastructure — LangChain-based systems, agentic architectures, integration layers
- Cloud-native strategy and implementation — AWS, Google Cloud, Kubernetes, real-time data pipelines

## Engagement modes

- **Hands-on build lead**: senior capacity that ships code alongside the in-house team
- **0→1 architecture and stack selection**: greenfield system design, build-versus-buy, technology selection
- **AI integration and development**: LangChain-based agents, retrieval pipelines, inference layers, greenfield agentic systems
- **Compliance-first system design**: GDPR, DORA, MiCA, EU AI Act-aligned architecture
- **Crisis recovery**: rescue of stalled projects, post-incident rebuilds, infrastructure remediation
- **Engineering team scaling and hiring**: team structure, hiring loops, senior network access
- **Technical due diligence**: pre-investment review for VCs, family offices, and acquirers
- **Advisor**: monthly retainer with board attendance and technical input

## Commercial terms

- **Minimum engagement**: 1 day per week
- **Pricing**: day rate, weekly retainer, or monthly retainer (rates on request)
- **Typical length**: 3–6 months for build engagements; 1–4 weeks for due diligence; ongoing for advisor retainers
- **Time zones**: engagements across North America, EMEA, and Asia are routine

## Technology stack

- **Languages**: Go, Python, C++, Node.js, SQL
- **Data**: SQL and NoSQL databases, vector databases (pgvector, Pinecone, Weaviate), Apache Kafka
- **Infrastructure**: Docker, Kubernetes, Nginx, NATS JetStream, Grafana
- **Cloud**: Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud Platform (GCP), DigitalOcean
- **AI tooling**: Claude, LangChain, TensorFlow

- [Work](https://cuko.uk/work): selected engagements and recurring engagement shapes
- [About](https://cuko.uk/about): operator background, stack, operating principles
- [Contact](https://cuko.uk/contact): channels and engaging Cuko Ltd
- [Accessibility](https://cuko.uk/accessibility): WCAG 2.2 AA conformance statement
- [Privacy](https://cuko.uk/privacy): UK GDPR and EU GDPR notice for cuko.uk
- [Security](https://cuko.uk/security): RFC 9116 disclosure policy
- [Blog](https://cuko.uk/blog): engineering essays on architecture, latency, real-time systems, and fractional CTO practice

## Blog posts

- [When you actually need nanoseconds](https://cuko.uk/blog/when-you-actually-need-nanoseconds/): When software genuinely needs sub-millisecond or microsecond latency, how to identify which latency regime you are in, the three signals that justify dropping a regime, and the order-of-magnitude cost of crossing each wall (web, real-time-ish, sub-millisecond, microsecond, hardware-bound, FPGA/ASIC). Published 2026-04-26.
- [What DORA actually requires from your ICT risk management framework](https://cuko.uk/blog/what-dora-actually-requires/): Practitioner's guide to the engineering work the Digital Operational Resilience Act (Regulation (EU) 2022/2554) actually forces on financial entities and CASPs — five engineering pillars (ICT risk management Arts 6–16, incident management Arts 17–23, resilience testing Arts 24–26, third-party risk Arts 28–30, exit strategies Art 28(8)), cost tiers, common failure modes, and the fractional CTO playbook for shipping framework as code. Published 2026-05-26.
- [EU AI Act Article 12 in production: what tamper-evident logging actually means](https://cuko.uk/blog/eu-ai-act-article-12-tamper-evident-logging/): Practitioner's guide to Article 12 of the EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) for providers of high-risk AI systems under Annex III. What "automatic recording over the lifetime of the system" actually requires, why tamper-evident is not encrypted, four implementation tiers from "we have logs" to externally anchored verifiable ledgers, and the four other articles that depend on Article 12 logs. Deadline 2 August 2026. Published 2026-06-16.
- [CLARITY Act vs MiCA: what CTOs do now](https://cuko.uk/blog/clarity-act-vs-mica-what-ctos-do-now/): Senate Banking Committee marks up H.R. 3633 (Digital Asset Market Clarity Act of 2025) on 14 May 2026. The bill splits SEC and CFTC jurisdiction over digital assets using a four-part "mature blockchain system" test (open source, transparent rules, no single controller, no 20%+ holder). MiCA (Regulation (EU) 2023/1114) already regulates both ends functionally with CASP authorisation since 30 December 2024. Four engineering moves for crypto CTOs with US and EU exposure: dual classification map, audit MiCA custody substrate for CLARITY-readiness, dual-output market-abuse surveillance, single canonical event log. Approximately 80% of MiCA engineering work transfers. Published 2026-05-13.
